action.reasoning: Observation: The hotspot one-hot MLP baseline is fast (~28s train+smoke) and gives decent validation rho (~0.857) but much lower hidden-test rho (~0.573), indicating overreliance on simple mutation identity features and poor extrapolation to >2-mutation combinations. Analysis: Since mutations are concentrated in a 28-aa window, a stronger yet still cheap model is to explicitly model interactions within that window. A pairwise epistasis linear model over amino-acid identities in the hotspot can generalize combinatorial effects better than an MLP on mostly sparse features. We can combine single-site and pairwise one-hot features, fit with ridge regression in closed form on CPU/GPU-friendly linear algebra, and keep prediction offline-safe. Decision: Implement a pairwise ridge regressor over the 28-aa hotspot plus mutation-count features, train/evaluate quickly, smoke-test predict, and submit eval.
